plan.title: Plan: Improve Fleet Widget Alignment and Progress Visibility
plan.overview:
This plan extends the existing fleet CLI widget so task rows render with stable
column widths and subagent values line up vertically, while also showing the
most recent progress update directly beneath each task row with a timestamp and
message. The work stays inside the current fleet extension and existing
task/progress files; it does not add new persistence formats, external
integrations, or authentication changes.

Because this is an internal tool change for a known team, the implementation
favors reliability and debuggability over novelty. The approach is to first
confirm how the widget currently formats rows and receives progress events, then
thread the latest progress timestamp and message through the existing runtime
state, and finally update the widget rendering, demo coverage, and tests so the
change is easy to validate before rollout.

The main design decision is to keep the widget as a fixed-width text table
rendered through `ctx.ui.setWidget()` in `extensions/fleet/widget.ts` rather
than introducing a new custom TUI component. Scope is limited to fleet
rendering, progress metadata plumbing, tests, and lightweight operator
documentation; it does not redesign `/fleet:status`, change orchestrator
scheduling behavior, or introduce any new long-lived state beyond the current
`progress.jsonl` and `state.json` data already used by fleet.
